mission.title: grpc: [Python] Multi-thread support for python asyncio gRPC clients
mission.description: [Python] Multi-thread support for python asyncio gRPC clients. This issue affects 39+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Check your build output and test logs for errors related to this issue.

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
This issue was created at the request of  on [a stackoverflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65945944/multi-thread-support-for-python-asyncio-grpc-clients).

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Python] Multi-thread support for python asyncio gRPC…
steps.description:
I hit this as well, and the code is in a library I'm consuming so the workaround isn't as helpful as one might hope. I might try to implement it for them in the meantime. On my mac I was able to use multiprocessing to work around this however when going to deploy to linux I learned it behaves just
```yaml
2021-01-27 09:33:56,937 ERROR [asyncio] [thread_0] Exception in callback PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events()()
handle: )()>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.8/asyncio/events.py", line 81, in _run
    self._context.run(self._callback, *self._args)
  File "src/python/grpcio/grpc/_cython/_cygrpc/aio/completion_queue.pyx.pxi", line 147, in grpc._cython.cygrpc.PollerCompletionQueue._handle_events
BlockingIOError: [Errno 11] Resource temporarily unavailable
2021-01-27 09:33:56,937 ERROR [asyncio] [thread_1] Exception in callback PollerCompletionQueue._handle_event
```

steps.title: Upgrade grpc to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade grpc:
Update the grpc dependency in your project manifest to the latest version.
See the project's releases: https://github.com/grpc/grpc/releases

steps.title: Confirm [Python] Multi-thread support for python asyncio… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: I hit this as well, and the code is in a library I'm consuming so the workaround isn't as helpful as one might hope. I might try to implement it for them in the meantime. On my mac I was able to use multiprocessing to work around this however when going to deploy to linux I learned it behaves just as multi-threading on mac did for some reason.
